name: Proposal & Business Case Deck
description: Late-stage sale. Proposal submission, executive readout, or board approval support. Goal is to win the commercial decision.
audience: Economic Buyer + C-suite + procurement + sometimes board members
tone: Executive, evidence-rich, financially literate. Every claim is supported by a number or a citation.

slides.content_guidance: Customer + Proposal Title + Version + Date
Recommendation in one sentence + 3 key reasons in supporting bullets
The customer's current state, in their own numbers
Macro pressure / regulatory / competitive trigger (use icons if 2–3 themes)
Pain theme 1 as separate slide with supporting numbers
Pain theme 2 as separate slide with supporting numbers
Pain theme 3 as separate slide with supporting numbers
Quantified target state (before/after format)
Solution architecture at a high level (workflow diagram)

slides.purpose: Solution — How It Solves Each Pain
slides.content_guidance: Pain → Solution mapping, one row per pain (3 rows = 3 pains)
3–5 specific differentiators, each with proof (not generic claims)
Phases, durations, key milestones
Named team + experience + RACI (use photo grid + responsibilities)
Pricing breakdown — software / services / support. Currency clearly stated (THB / USD / EUR). Show net + VAT for Thai customers
3-year or 5-year TCO with line items (use stacked bar chart if data-heavy)
Quantified benefits, payback period, NPV/IRR if relevant. Never invent benefits — use customer statements or industry benchmarks with citation
Top 5 risks + mitigation for each (honest assessment, not vague 'low risk' claims)
Logos with one-line outcomes (1–3 references)
One number that proves the firm can deliver (e.g., '500+ implementations in 18 months')

slides.purpose: What We Need to Move Forward
slides.content_guidance: Decision needed + by when
Deeper methodology / framework explanation
Full org structure with team members
Line-by-line cost breakdown
Detailed security certifications and service level agreements
For Thai government RFPs only: compliance with each TOR section by paragraph number

special_notes.thai_government_bids: When responding to a TOR/RFP for Thai government: Slide 1 must show TOR reference number and submission date. Slides 14–16 must follow Thai government cost categories from พรบ. and ระเบียบ (see govt-egp-gfmis skill). Add Appendix slide listing compliance with each TOR section by paragraph number.
special_notes.currency_requirement: Investment Summary (Slide 14) must state currency explicitly (THB / USD / EUR). For Thai customers, show net + VAT separately.
special_notes.roi_requirement: Never invent benefits. Every benefit line item must come from: (a) a real customer statement, (b) an industry benchmark with citation, or (c) a vendor-published case study (cited).
